name: arbhar
description: Granular processor with six 10-second buffers, two grain engines, 1V/oct pitch tracking, onboard capture and onset detection, and mono or stereo input paths. Built-in microphone and USB sample import turn it into a live recorder as well as an effect.

controls.name: Input Level
controls.description: Sets input gain before the limiter. Around 10 o'clock is unity; clockwise drives the preamp.
controls.detail: unity at ~10:00 · analogue limiting on high levels

controls.name: Dry/Wet
controls.description: Crossfades between the dry input and the granular engines.

controls.name: Output Level
controls.description: Sets the level at both outputs.

controls.name: Capture
controls.description: Starts and stops recording to the selected layer.
controls.detail: press to record · press again to stop

controls.name: Shift
controls.description: Accesses secondary functions such as capture modes, track-and-hold, and mode selection.
controls.detail: holds secondary state · used with Capture and Layer

controls.name: Layer
controls.description: Selects one of the six layers, or omega to link them through Scan.
controls.detail: alpha through zeta · omega = scan all layers

controls.name: Intensity
controls.description: Sets grain density and asynchronous versus synchronous grain generation.
controls.detail: centre = max grains · extremes disable continuous engine

controls.name: Strike
controls.description: Generates a single accent grain from the strike engine.
controls.detail: button and trigger input both fire a grain

controls.name: Grain Window
controls.description: Shapes the grain envelope from square to Gaussian to sawtooth.
controls.detail: CCW: square · noon: Gaussian · CW: descending saw

controls.name: Grain Direction
controls.description: Sets the probability of forward versus reverse playback.

controls.name: Length
controls.detail: ~4 ms to 3 s · center around 750 ms

controls.name: Scan
controls.description: Sets the playback position inside the active layer.
controls.detail: sets grain start position at grain onset

controls.name: Spray
controls.detail: CCW: tight cluster · CW: fully random layer access

controls.name: Pitch Deviation
controls.description: Adds per-grain random or quantized pitch offsets.
controls.detail: CCW: unquantized random · CW: quantized intervals

controls.name: Sensitivity
controls.description: Sets onset detector sensitivity or Input 2 level in stereo mode.

controls.name: Hold
controls.description: Sets the minimum time between onset detections or recorded events.

controls.name: Dub
controls.description: Sets sound-on-sound attenuation for overdub capture.
controls.detail: CCW: replace · noon: half-old/half-new · CW: full layering

inputs.name: Input
inputs.description: AC-coupled audio input for capture and playback processing.

inputs.name: Onset Input
inputs.description: Audio input for onset detection and stereo capture analysis.

inputs.name: Capture Pulse Input
inputs.description: Gate or trigger input that starts, stops, or retriggers capture.

inputs.name: Intensity CV Input
inputs.description: CV over grain density and amplitude distribution.

inputs.name: Strike Input
inputs.description: Trigger or gate input for the strike granular engine.

inputs.name: 1V/Octave Input
inputs.description: Pitch tracking input for grain transposition.

outputs.name: Output 1
outputs.description: Audio output. Grains are coin-tossed between both outputs by default.

outputs.name: Output 2
outputs.description: Second audio output. Unpatched, it normalizes to Output 1 for mono playback.

outputs.name: Pulse Output
outputs.description: Trigger or gate output from the onset engine.

behaviors.name: Two grain engines
behaviors.trigger: Intensity above minimum or Strike input
behaviors.description: The continuous engine runs from Length and Intensity, while the strike engine fires on Capture Button or Strike Input. Together they can generate up to 88 grains polyphonically.

behaviors.name: Layered capture
behaviors.description: arbhar records into one of six 10-second layers. Accumulative capture can pause and resume within a layer, turning one buffer into a composed collage.

behaviors.name: Scan and follow
behaviors.description: In omega, the Scan knob addresses all layers through position. In follow mode, the playback head tracks the record head, keeping grains anchored to captured motion.

behaviors.name: Onset-driven capture
behaviors.description: Onset analysis can arm capture and also generate triggers or gates from detected attacks. The Pulse Output becomes a usable event stream for external timing.

behaviors.name: Pitch deviation
behaviors.description: Counterclockwise adds random pitch drift. Clockwise adds quantized interval offsets, so each grain can move in a repeatable harmonic family instead of pure noise.

behaviors.name: Stereo and mono paths
behaviors.description: With only Output 1 patched, Output 2 normalizes to mono. Patching Output 2 opens stereo imaging and lets each side take independent grain distribution.

behaviors.name: Audio capture from the panel mic
behaviors.trigger: no cable at Input or Onset Input
behaviors.description: The onboard condenser microphone normalizes into the capture path. The module becomes a self-contained field recorder without external cabling.

patchIdeas.name: Frozen cloud
patchIdeas.patch: Capture a slow pad into layer alpha, then play it back as a stable granular cloud with stereo spread.
steps: Press Layer until alpha's LED lights.
Patch a slow pad → Input. Press Capture, hold ~3 seconds of source, press Capture again to stop.
Set Intensity ~noon (dense continuous grains).
Set Length ~1 o'clock (~500 ms grains) and Grain Window noon (Gaussian, smooth fades).
Set Scan ~noon and Spray ~10 o'clock for a tight but moving cluster.
Set Pitch at unity and Dry/Wet ~3 o'clock (mostly wet).
Patch Output 1 → Mixer L and Output 2 → Mixer R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Output 1 / Output 2 — stereo audio (~10 Vpp). Grains are coin-tossed between channels for a wide image.
patchIdeas.hear: The pad becomes a sustained, slowly drifting cloud that doesn't loop identically — grain start positions wander around Scan and pan randomly between L and R.
patchIdeas.diagram:
arbhar:
* Layer: alpha
* Intensity: noon
* Length: ~1 o'clock
* Scan: noon
* Dry/Wet: ~3 o'clock

VCO:

Mixer:

- VCO (Out) -> arbhar (Input)
- arbhar (Output 1) -> Mixer (L)
- arbhar (Output 2) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Onset-driven stutter
patchIdeas.patch: Let arbhar listen to a drum loop, fire grains on every detected attack, and clock an external envelope from the Pulse Output.
steps: Patch a drum loop → Input (also normalises to Onset Input).
Capture ~4 seconds into layer alpha (press Capture, then Capture again).
Set Sensitivity ~2 o'clock so quiet hits still trigger.
Set Hold ~9 o'clock (~50 ms minimum gap between detections).
Set Length ~10 o'clock (~100 ms short grains) and Intensity ~10 o'clock.
Patch Pulse Output → external Envelope (Trig) to fire a VCA in sync with detected hits.
Patch Output 1 → Mixer L, Output 2 → Mixer R, Dry/Wet ~2 o'clock.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Output 1 / Output 2 — granular stutter mixed with dry. Pulse Output emits a +5 V trigger on each detected onset.
patchIdeas.hear: Every drum hit punches a fresh grain out of the buffer; the external envelope opens in lockstep, so an outside voice ducks or fires along with the rhythm.
patchIdeas.diagram:
arbhar:
* Sensitivity: ~2 o'clock
* Hold: ~9 o'clock
* Length: ~10 o'clock

Drum Loop:
Envelope:
Mixer:

- Drum Loop (Out) -> arbhar (Input)
- arbhar (Pulse Output) t> Envelope (Trig)
- arbhar (Output 1) -> Mixer (L)
- arbhar (Output 2) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Omega wavetable sweep
patchIdeas.patch: Link all six layers under omega and sweep a slow LFO across the whole bank as one long wavetable.
steps: Capture different short sources into layers alpha through zeta (cycle Layer + Capture six times).
Press Layer until omega is selected (all six LEDs lit).
Set Length ~9 o'clock (~50 ms grains), Grain Window noon (Gaussian).
Set Grain Direction noon (50/50 forward/reverse), Pitch at unity.
Patch a slow bipolar LFO (~0.1 Hz) → Scan CV Input.
Set Scan ~noon so the LFO sweeps the full layer chain.
Set Intensity ~1 o'clock and Dry/Wet full CW.
Patch Output 1 → Output L, Output 2 → Output R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Output 1 / Output 2 — stereo wavetable-style sweep through all six captured layers.
patchIdeas.hear: A slow, evolving timbre that morphs across all six samples like one long wavetable. As the LFO rises, you cross from layer alpha's material into zeta's; turn Spray up to scramble within each layer.
patchIdeas.diagram:
arbhar:
* Layer: omega
* Grain Window: noon
* Grain Direction: noon
* Length: ~9 o'clock

LFO:

Output:

- LFO (Slow) >> arbhar (Scan CV Input)
- arbhar (Output 1) -> Output (L)
- arbhar (Output 2) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Dub sound-on-sound smear
patchIdeas.patch: Use the Dub knob to layer fresh captures over old ones, smearing a saw drone into a thick wash.
steps: Press Layer until alpha is selected.
Patch a saw VCO → Input.
Set Dub just past noon (half-old / half-new on each new capture pass).
Press Capture and let ~5 seconds record, then press Capture again.
Repeat capture 3–4 times — each pass blends new audio with the previous content.
Set Intensity ~10 o'clock (low continuous engine), Length ~2 o'clock (long grains).
Set Pitch at unity, Dry/Wet ~2 o'clock.
Patch Output 1 → Output (In).
patchIdeas.signalOut: Output 1 — mono mix of dry saw plus smeared granular layers (Output 2 unpatched normalises into Output 1).
patchIdeas.hear: Each capture pass thickens the buffer instead of replacing it, so the saw becomes a layered, blurred drone with the original still audible underneath the wet grains.
patchIdeas.diagram:
arbhar:
* Layer: alpha
* Dub: just past noon
* Intensity: ~10 o'clock
* Length: ~2 o'clock

VCO:
Output:

- VCO (Saw) -> arbhar (Input)
- arbhar (Output 1) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Strike percussion accents
patchIdeas.patch: Fire one short pitched grain per sequencer step with the strike engine, sitting on top of a dry rhythm.
steps: Capture ~3 seconds of any tonal source into layer alpha.
Turn Intensity full CCW to disable the continuous engine — only strikes will sound.
Set Length ~10 o'clock (~80 ms), Grain Window noon.
Set Pitch at −12 semitones (one octave down from unity).
Set Pitch Deviation ~2 o'clock (CW = quantised intervals per grain).
Patch a sequencer Gate → Strike Input. Each gate fires one accent grain.
Set Dry/Wet ~2 o'clock so the dry rhythm stays audible.
Patch Output 1 → Output (In).
patchIdeas.signalOut: Output 1 — dry source plus one short pitched grain per Strike trigger. Output 2 unpatched mirrors Output 1.
patchIdeas.hear: A pitched percussive accent on every step, transposed an octave down with quantised pitch jumps from Pitch Deviation. Continuous engine stays silent, so the rhythm is purely strike-driven.
patchIdeas.diagram:
arbhar:
* Layer: alpha
* Intensity: full CCW
* Pitch: -12 st
* Pitch Deviation: ~2 o'clock

Sequencer:
Output:

- Sequencer (Gate) t> arbhar (Strike Input)
- arbhar (Output 1) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: On-board mic field recorder
patchIdeas.patch: Use the panel microphone with no input cable to capture room sound, then play it back through the granular engine.
steps: Leave Input and Onset Input unpatched — the on-module microphone normalises into the capture path.
Press Layer until alpha is selected.
Set Input Level ~11 o'clock and tap near the module to test the mic LED responds.
Press Capture, speak / play / scrape near the module for ~5 seconds, press Capture to stop.
Set Length ~12 o'clock (~750 ms), Intensity ~1 o'clock, Spray ~11 o'clock.
Set Scan slowly turning by hand or patch a slow LFO → Scan CV Input.
Set Dry/Wet full CW (wet only) and patch Output 1 / Output 2 → Mixer L / R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Output 1 / Output 2 — granular playback of the mic recording. No external source, no cables on the input side.
patchIdeas.hear: Whatever you recorded acoustically (voice, keys, a tap on the case) comes back as a granular cloud you can scrub through with Scan. Useful as a self-contained sketchpad with no other modules patched in.
patchIdeas.diagram:
arbhar:
* Input: (unpatched - mic active)
* Layer: alpha
* Length: ~12 o'clock
* Dry/Wet: full CW

Mixer:

- (panel mic) -> arbhar (Input - normalised)
- arbhar (Output 1) -> Mixer (L)
- arbhar (Output 2) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: 1V/oct melodic granular voice
patchIdeas.patch: Capture a held tone, then play arbhar as a pitched instrument tracked by a sequencer's 1V/oct.
steps: Patch a sustained VCO drone → Input.
Capture ~2 seconds into layer alpha, then press Capture again to stop.
Patch a sequencer 1V/oct → 1V/Octave Input (tracks grain transposition).
Patch the sequencer Gate → Strike Input (one grain per note).
Turn Intensity full CCW so only strikes sound.
Set Pitch at unity (your sequencer drives pitch from 0 V).
Set Length ~11 o'clock (~150 ms), Grain Window noon.
Set Dry/Wet full CW, Output 1 → Mixer L, Output 2 → Mixer R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Output 1 / Output 2 — melodic granular voice. One pitched grain per sequencer gate, transposed by 1V/oct.
patchIdeas.hear: A plucky, vocal-ish lead that follows your sequence note for note. Sweep Scan to change the timbre source from inside the captured drone; raise Pitch Deviation CW for harmonised stacks.
patchIdeas.diagram:
arbhar:
* Layer: alpha
* Intensity: full CCW
* Pitch: unity
* Length: ~11 o'clock

- VCO (Drone) -> arbhar (Input)
- Sequencer (1V/oct) p> arbhar (1V/Octave Input)
- Sequencer (Gate) t> arbhar (Strike Input)
- arbhar (Output 1) -> Mixer (L)
- arbhar (Output 2) -> Mixer (R)
